title: bug_overlay_clobber: overlay auto-commit could commit memory OVER the project repo (−305k) — fixed with a memory-only staged-set guard + standalone-repo init (v0.38.3)
context: A real, contained incident on a user's repo: in shared+commit mode, the Hunch post-commit auto-sync committed the private overlay store over the project repo (−305k lines) and pushed it. User disabled the hook, reset+force-pushed the good commit, audited all branches — contained to the branch tip, fully restored. MCP recording/query were unaffected (overlay-only); only the auto-commit hook was broken.
decision: Root cause: commitAndPushHunch(hunchDir) runs `git -C hunchDir add -- . / commit / pull --no-rebase --autostash / push` and TRUSTED that hunchDir is a dedicated overlay git repo. When the overlay was never its own repo (a plain .hunch-private dir from `hunch shared` with no --repo), `git -C` walks UP to the PROJECT repo, so those destructive ops (esp. pull --autostash + push) hit the user's code. Fix (defense in depth): (1) RUNTIME BACKSTOP in commitAndPushHunch — a memory sync is purely additive JSON, so refuse to commit if the staged set has ANY deletion/rename or non-.json file (unstage + bail, no commit, no push), and only pull/push after a real memory commit was created. New helper stagedIsMemoryOnly() inspects `git diff --cached --name-status`. (2) SETUP GUARD in configureOverlay — when --auto-commit is on but the overlay root isn't its own git repo, `git init` it standalone so git can't escape to the project. test/overlay-commit-guard.test.ts locks both (refuses deletion, refuses mixed non-JSON, commits clean JSON). Full suite 359 pass. Shipped 0.38.3.
consequences: INVARIANT (do not remove): the overlay auto-commit must NEVER commit a staged set containing a deletion or a non-.json file — a memory sync is additive JSON only. The guard in commitAndPushHunch (stagedIsMemoryOnly) and its test are the enforcement; removing either re-opens a data-loss bug.

Any future change to commitAndPushHunch / the overlay sync must preserve: (a) only-push-after-a-real-commit, and (b) the memory-only staged-set check. The `git add -- .` is cwd-scoped so it alone can't stage the whole repo, but pull --autostash + push on the enclosing repo is the lethal part — the guard now blocks the commit that precedes them.

Severity: data-loss grade, and it was LIVE on npm (0.36–0.38.2 had the unsafe path). 0.38.3 is the fix; users on `hunch shared/private --auto-commit` should upgrade. The default (no --auto-commit) and MCP record/query were never affected.

Honest reflection for the launch narrative: Hunch is positioned as the tool that PREVENTS AI/automation from breaking your repo, and its own auto-commit hook nearly destroyed one. The git-native design is exactly why it was fully recoverable (reset + force-push), which is a point in favor of git-native memory — but the auto-commit feature shipped without this guard and a test, which is the real lesson.
